task_title: Dev Infra Bootstrap — Repo Scaffold, Secrets, Receipt Storage, ConfigMap
expansion_prompt: Break this infrastructure bootstrap task into 6 subtasks covering these distinct work streams: (1) Anchor project scaffolding — repo init, directory structure, Cargo.toml/package.json configuration, tooling config (biome.json, .tool-versions, .gitignore); (2) SeaweedFS receipt bucket provisioning — bucket creation via S3 API or weed shell, K8s Job/init script in infra/gitops/; (3) Operator keypair secrets pipeline — 1Password item creation, OpenBao sync config, ExternalSecret CR for K8s Secret cto-pay-operator-keypair; (4) ConfigMap creation — cto-pay-infra-endpoints with all 5 keys, separate ExternalSecret for Helius API key; (5) Devnet operator keypair generation — keygen, airdrop, 1Password storage, .gitignore entry; (6) Devnet setup script — scripts/setup-devnet.sh with airdrop, SPL mint creation, config/devnet.json output. Subtasks 1 can run in parallel with 2-4 (infra provisioning). Subtask 5 depends on 3. Subtask 6 depends on 1 and 5. Assign implementer subagents for all 6.
reasoning: This task spans 6 distinct deliverables across multiple infrastructure systems (Git/Anchor CLI, SeaweedFS S3, 1Password, OpenBao, K8s External Secrets Operator, ConfigMaps, Solana CLI). Each subsystem has its own tooling and validation requirements. The breadth of integration points and the fact that all downstream tasks depend on this make it a high-complexity architectural bootstrap task.

task_title: Anchor Program — Account Structures, Errors, Constants, and initialize_operator
expansion_prompt: Break this Anchor program foundation task into 4 subtasks: (1) State module — implement OperatorConfig, CustomerBalance, TaskReceipt structs with INIT_SPACE, TaskStatus enum, and mod.rs; include unit tests validating INIT_SPACE matches manual byte calculations; (2) Constants and errors — implement constants.rs (SLOTS_PER_DAY, PDA seeds) and errors.rs (full CtoPayError enum with 12 variants); (3) initialize_operator instruction — implement the instruction handler and Accounts struct with vault PDA creation, mint parameter storage, protocol_fee_bps validation, and token account initialization via anchor_spl; (4) lib.rs wiring and build verification — declare_id!, module imports, program module with initialize_operator entry point, clippy::pedantic configuration, verify anchor build produces correct IDL. Subtasks 1 and 2 can run in parallel. Subtask 3 depends on 1 and 2. Subtask 4 depends on 3. Assign implementer subagents for all.
reasoning: Defines 3 PDA account structures with precise byte layouts, a 12-variant error enum, constants, and the initialize_operator instruction with vault token account creation. While well-specified, it involves multiple interrelated files, SPL token account initialization via CPI, PDA derivation patterns, and strict clippy compliance — enough complexity across the data model foundation to warrant decomposition.

task_title: Anchor Program — Customer Balance Operations: create, deposit, withdraw, update_spending_caps
expansion_prompt: Break this into 4 subtasks, one per instruction: (1) create_customer_account — PDA initialization with customer seed, pause check, cap validation (max_per_task <= max_per_day), Clock sysvar for created_at and daily_reset_slot; (2) deposit — SPL token CPI transfer from customer to vault, mint validation against operator_config.mint, pause check, checked arithmetic for balance/total_deposited updates; (3) withdraw — SPL token CPI transfer from vault to customer using vault PDA authority signer seeds, NO pause check (safety valve), balance sufficiency check, mint validation; (4) update_spending_caps — customer-signed cap update with validation, no daily_spent reset. Key dependency: the vault authority PDA signing pattern in withdraw must match the vault creation pattern from Task 2's initialize_operator. All 4 subtasks can be developed in parallel since they share the same account structures but have independent logic. Register all instructions in lib.rs. Assign implementer subagents.
reasoning: Four distinct instructions each involving Anchor account validation macros, SPL token CPI patterns, and checked arithmetic. The withdraw instruction requires vault PDA authority signing which adds CPI complexity. The deliberate omission of pause checks on withdraw is a security-critical design decision that must be implemented correctly. Cross-cutting mint validation pattern applies to three of the four instructions.

task_title: Anchor Program — Settlement Engine: settle_task, refund_task, pause, unpause
expansion_prompt: Break this into 5 subtasks: (1) settle_task instruction — the most complex instruction with 5-step validation chain (pause, zero amount, per-task cap, daily cap with slot-based reset logic, balance check), fee computation (amount * bps / 10000 with checked math), SPL CPI vault→treasury transfer, CustomerBalance debit, TaskReceipt PDA initialization; (2) refund_task instruction — ledger-only refund (no SPL transfer), status guard (must be Settled), balance credit, total_spent decrement, status→Refunded, NO pause check; (3) pause and unpause instructions — simple authority-gated boolean toggle on OperatorConfig, implemented together as they are trivial; (4) lib.rs integration — register all new instructions (bringing total to 9), verify full IDL generation with all instructions, accounts, and args; (5) Documentation pass — doc comments on every public function and struct explaining purpose and security model, verify program binary size < 1.4MB. Subtasks 1-3 can run in parallel. Subtask 4 depends on 1-3. Subtask 5 depends on 4. Critical: settle_task daily cap reset uses slot arithmetic (SLOTS_PER_DAY=216000), not wall-clock time.
reasoning: settle_task is the most complex instruction in the entire program with a 5-step validation chain, daily cap reset logic using slot arithmetic, fee computation with specific multiply-first-then-divide ordering, SPL token CPI, and multi-field state updates. refund_task has a nuanced ledger-only accounting model (no token transfer) that requires careful documentation. Combined, these complete the full program — making integration verification critical.

task_title: Anchor Program Tests — Happy-Path Integration Suite
expansion_prompt: Break this into 4 subtasks: (1) Test infrastructure — setup.ts and helpers.ts with Bankrun configuration, mint creation, token account helpers, initializeProgram helper, createCustomer helper, task_id_hash SHA-256 helper, standard test wallets (operator, treasury, customer1, customer2); (2) Initialize and customer lifecycle tests — initialize.test.ts (OperatorConfig creation, vault setup, PDA determinism) and customer-lifecycle.test.ts (create account, single/multiple deposits, partial/full withdraw, update caps); (3) Settlement and refund tests — settlement.test.ts (single settle, multi settle, fee verification, daily_spent tracking, daily cap reset via warpToSlot, receipt field verification) and refund.test.ts (refund + withdraw refunded funds); (4) Full end-to-end loop test — full-loop.test.ts matching the demo video sequence (init→create→deposit→settle→verify→withdraw with exact amounts at 6 decimal precision). Subtask 1 must complete first. Subtasks 2-4 can run in parallel once helpers are available. All tests use Bankrun for sub-30s execution. Assign tester subagents.
reasoning: Comprehensive test suite requiring Bankrun test infrastructure setup, multiple helper functions, and 15+ test cases spanning 5 files. The settlement tests require Bankrun's warpToSlot for daily cap reset verification. The full-loop test must match exact demo video amounts with 6-decimal precision. While each individual test is straightforward, the combined scope of infrastructure plus coverage warrants decomposition.

task_title: Anchor Program Tests — Edge Cases, Error Paths, and Security Scenarios
expansion_prompt: Break this into 4 subtasks aligned with test file groupings: (1) spending-caps.test.ts — 7 tests for per-task cap exceeded, daily cap exceeded, cumulative daily overflow, post-reset success, cap boundary exact-match, updated caps behavior; (2) authorization.test.ts and pause-behavior.test.ts — 7 auth failure tests (wrong signer for settle/refund/pause/unpause/withdraw/update_caps, wrong customer PDA) plus 7 pause tests (create/deposit/settle blocked, withdraw/refund succeed, unpause resumes, only operator can toggle); (3) overflow-edge.test.ts and double-operations.test.ts — 7+4 tests covering u64::MAX deposits, near-overflow total_spent, fee computation overflow, zero amount failures, duplicate customer account, duplicate task_id_hash settlement, double refund; (4) validation.test.ts — 5 tests for invalid fee bps, wrong mint, invalid cap ratios, insufficient balance withdraw/settle. Critical requirement: every test must assert the specific CtoPayError variant, not just transaction failure. All subtasks can run in parallel. Assign tester subagents.
reasoning: 30+ negative test cases requiring precise error code assertion across 6 security dimensions. Each test category requires understanding specific Anchor error propagation patterns. The spending cap boundary tests require careful arithmetic setup. The pause behavior tests must verify the asymmetric pause policy (withdraw/refund succeed while paused). Overflow tests need pre-seeded near-max values. The breadth of security scenarios justifies decomposition by vulnerability class.

task_title: Solana Program Security Audit — Account Validation, Signer Checks, Arithmetic Safety
expansion_prompt: Break this security audit into 3 subtasks: (1) Automated static analysis — run anchor verify for bytecode-source match, run Soteria or cargo-audit for known vulnerability patterns, run clippy with security-focused lints, check for unsafe blocks, verify no init_if_needed usage, scan for raw arithmetic (non-checked +/-/*/÷ on u64); (2) Manual code review — systematic review across Solana vulnerability classes: account validation (has_one constraints, PDA seed uniqueness, owner checks), signer verification (all authority-gated instructions), arithmetic safety (checked_* usage completeness, multiply-before-divide ordering), PDA collision analysis (seed entropy for task_receipt and customer_balance), rent exemption (all init accounts), reentrancy (CPI ordering — state updates before transfers), pause bypass analysis (verify only withdraw/refund bypass pause); (3) Findings report — produce severity-rated findings document (Critical/High/Medium/Low/Informational) with specific file:line references, reproduction steps, and remediation guidance for each finding. Subtasks 1 and 2 can run in parallel. Subtask 3 depends on both. Assign reviewer subagents.
reasoning: Systematic security review covering multiple Solana-specific vulnerability classes (account validation, signer checks, PDA collisions, arithmetic overflow, reentrancy via CPI ordering). The task description appears truncated but the scope is clear from the title and available details. Requires both automated tooling and manual review expertise. The findings report needs structured severity ratings and remediation guidance — standard for security audit deliverables.
